title: leptobalanus stevensii
intro_study: According to "International journal of leprosy and other mycobacterial diseases : official organ of the International Leprosy Association", leptobalanus stevensii (also known as oil of hydnocarpus) has been found to have a beneficial effect on wound healing, increasing the strength of scar tissue and collagen.
side_effects: Leptobalanus stevensii can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high toxicity levels. Prolonged exposure to its sap or extracts can also cause skin irritation, rashes, and allergic reactions in some individuals. In severe cases, ingesting large amounts of the plant's toxic compounds can lead to respiratory failure, seizures, and even death. Furthermore, its improper use can also interfere with certain medications, exacerbating underlying health conditions.
